title: Recombinant protein expression and purification of Taq DNA polymerase
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8qm5s","text":"This is a slightly modified and simplified version of a protocol by Thomas G.W. Graham et al, which is available at https://gitlab.com/tjian-darzacq-lab/bearmix and has been described in depth in the article 10.1371/journal.pone.0246647, for the recombinant expression of a E602D mutant of Taq DNA polymerase in pET-28a that is available in Addgene (Addgene plasmid # 166944 ; http://n2t.net/addgene:166944 ; RRID:Addgene_166944).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":208,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":116,"length":44},{"key":1,"offset":208,"length":28},{"key":2,"offset":377,"length":29}],"data":{}},{"key":"8ugku","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6c67s","text":"The main goal of this protocol is to eliminate the use of large volumes for dialysis and potential issues with the protein crashing out of the solution due to the use of concentrators for buffer exchange of this enzyme into storage conditions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"0325CB1E2C2111EC8E6C0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://gitlab.com/tjian-darzacq-lab/bearmix"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"EEBF75252C2011EC8E6C0A58A9FEAC02","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0246647"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"343C800A2C2111EC8E6C0A58A9FEAC02","url":"http://n2t.net/addgene:166944"}}}}

steps: Transform 100 ng of plasmid containing Taq DNA polymerase into E. coli C41 competent cells using either heat shock or electroporation.
Spread transformed cells in LB Agar plates supplemented with 0.05 mg/mL Kan. Grow plate overnight at 37 °C .
Select a single colony from the LB agar plate to prepare a preinoculum in 10 mL LB media supplemented with 0.05 mg/mL Kan. Grow overnight at 200 rpm, 37 °C, .

Use the full volume of the preinoculum to inoculate 1 L of LB (or TB) media supplemented with 0.05 mg/mL Kan (1% inoculation). Grow at 160 rpm, 37 °C, until reaching an optical density at 600 nm (OD 600 ) = 0.8.
Upon reaching OD 600 = 0.8, add IPTG to a final concentration of 0.5 millimolar (mM) and incubate 160 rpm, 37 °C, 2 hours .
Centrifuge the cell culture 4000 x g, 4 °C, 20 minutes .Then, resuspend the cell pellet in 50 mL of Buffer A freshly supplemented with 1.0 millimolar (mM) PMSF and 0.2 mg/mL lysozyme.
Incubate the resuspended cells at 80 rpm, Room temperature, 20 minutes .
Sonicate on ice for 00:08:00 using cycles of 00:00:01 ON and 00:00:06 OFF at 40% amplitude (Qsonica Q125, 125W).

On an ultracentrifugation tube, incubate the unclarified lysate at 75 °C for 00:30:00 to precipitate most of E. coli proteins, and then place on ice for 00:05:00 . Centrifuge 20000 x g, 4 °C, 20 minutes and collect the supernatant. You might want to collect a small sample for SDS-PAGE afterwards.

On a 1 mL HisTrap column (GE Healthcare) pre-equilibrated with 10 column volumes (c.v.) (here, 10 mL) of Buffer A , load the supernant. Wash with 10-20 c.v. of Buffer B . Repeat the wash step with Buffer C . Then, elute with 5 c.v. of Buffer D , collecting the eluted fractions every 0.5 mL in 1.5 ml tubes.

This method was preferred over protein dyalisis or Amicon protein concentration to avoid using large buffer volumes and proteins crashing out of the solution. Dilute the pooled fractions 6X in buffer containing 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 and 100 mM NaCl.

For storage, supplement your pooled fractions with 100 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 0.2 millimolar (mM) EDTA and 6 millimolar (mM) DTT. Then, dilute the sample by adding glycerol up to 50 % volume to reach final storage conditions: 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, ~100 mM NaCl, 0.1 mM EDTA, 3 mM DTT.
Generate 200 µL aliquots of the enzyme and store it at -20 °C until required.

To quickly pool the fractions containing the protein of interest, prepare a 96-well plate or 1.5 mL tubes with 40 µL of 5X Bradford reagent and 150 µL of distilled water. Then, add 10 µL of each protein fraction and compare against a blank reference sample corresponding to 10 µL of Buffer C . You can determine your protein-containing fractions either by absorbance at 595 nm on a plate reader or visually by comparing the blue coloration of each fraction against the blank reference. Pool your fractions and collect a 10 µL sample for SDS-PAGE.

Next, load the diluted sample onto a 1 ml HiTrap Heparin column previously equilibrated with 10 c.v. (here, 10 mL) Buffer HA . Then, elute the protein using a 10 c.v. linear gradient against Buffer HB , collecting the eluted fractions every 0.5 mL in 1.5 ml tubes. This linear gradient can be achieved by connecting two containers, one with 5 c.v. Buffer HA and the other with 5 c.v. buffer HB, with a syphon or a tube, and withdrawing solution from the Buffer HA container to the column using a cheap peristaltic pump or by gravity.
Again, determine your protein-containing fractions using the Bradford assay. Pool your fractions and determine its protein concentration using the same method and collect a 10 µL sample for SDS-PAGE.
SDS-PAGE of all purification steps of Taq DNA polymerase. Pooled eluted fractions from IMAC and Heparin correspond to P1, HP1 and HP2.
